text:
There once was a boy named Max who had two very different parents. His mother, Stella, was an adventurous free spirit who never worried about mundane things like security fences or front gates. She believed in dancing through the forest at midnight and was constantly trying to teach Max how to roll through fire hydrants for fun.

On the other hand, Max's father was a meticulous accountant named Charles. If the phrase "stow away safely" ever echoed through their home, it was Charles's mantra. Charles had never seen Stella in anything but her sensible shoes. He worried about whether a dragon had buried treasure somewhere near their town and had an elaborate system for organizing his tax returns.

One summer afternoon, Max found a mysterious, glowing orb in the backyard, picked it up, and soon after, could only see in red. Panicked, he ran inside to tell Stella—where he immediately started laughing. "Are you trying to tell me you've been abducted by a blood-red dragon?" he joked.
